city_personality.slang_reaction: Aduh, Bapak ini bikin pusing kepala saja.

opening_sequence.title: Welcome to the Super Priority Destination, Bupati
opening_sequence.intro: Jakarta has declared your quiet fishing town a 'Super Priority' tourist hub. Now, luxury resorts have infinite clean water while the locals wait for water trucks, the harbor is choked with illegal liveaboards, and the Komodo dragons are getting stressed. Don't mess up the postcard.

advisors.name: Hendrikus Ndolu
advisors.hidden_agenda: Facilitating land grabs for a powerful syndicate of Jakarta-based hotel developers.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Implementing a strict moratorium on all new coastal luxury hotel developments.

briefing: The central government just released the next tranche of infrastructure funding.
Foreign investors are very interested in the empty plots near Waecicu Beach.
Local tax revenue is up, but most of the profits are flowing back to Jakarta.
agreement: A very profitable choice for all stakeholders.
The investors will be pleased with this certainty.
disagreement: You are scaring away the capital we desperately need.
Jakarta is watching, and they don't like this.
dialogue.betrayal: You cut off my investors. I've already arranged for Jakarta to slash your discretionary funding.
roast: You run this economy like a roadside warung.
Even the backpackers manage their money better than you.
scandal_reaction: We need to assure the investors that their assets are safe.
Let me handle the financial press.

advisors.name: Dr. Sari Wulan
advisors.hidden_agenda: Redirecting tourism levies directly to local micro-entrepreneur grants, bypassing Jakarta contractors.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Allowing over 60% of municipal tourism revenue to be siphoned to foreign resort conglomerates.

briefing: Local homestays and warungs are still paying the same taxes as international chains.
The tourism levy fund is bloated with unaccounted administrative fees.
Small businesses are defaulting on loans because Jakarta takes the bulk of the revenue.
agreement: Fair distribution. The local economy will finally breathe.
I'll draft the equity framework tonight.
disagreement: You're feeding Jakarta again while our markets starve.
This ignores the livelihoods of actual residents.
dialogue.betrayal: You let the resort monopolies drain our coffers. I'm funding a local recall campaign.
roast: Your economics are just creative accounting for absentee landlords.
You count foreign dollars but ignore local hunger.
scandal_reaction: I'll publish the full audit. Transparency is the only cure.
The numbers will speak louder than your denials.

advisors.name: AKBP Budi Santoso
advisors.hidden_agenda: Running a protection racket for unlicensed, substandard tourist Phinisi boats.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Launching a massive crackdown and impounding all unlicensed liveaboard vessels.

briefing: We have reports of illegal fishing inside the National Park boundaries again.
The tour guides are threatening to protest at the airport.
Harbor traffic is a mess, but my boys are keeping the peace.
agreement: A safe harbor is a profitable harbor.
Understood. We will secure the perimeter.
disagreement: You're stirring up a hornets' nest on the docks.
My officers aren't going to like this.
dialogue.betrayal: You sank my fleet. Good luck keeping order when the harbor mafia goes to war.
roast: Your security policy has more holes than a cheap wooden dinghy.
You couldn't police a kindergarten.
scandal_reaction: Keep the sirens on to drown out the journalists.
I'll double the guard at your residence.

advisors.name: Ir. Fajar Aditama
advisors.hidden_agenda: Securing permanent federal funding for Komodo National Park expansion, even if it restricts local fishing zones.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Legalizing destructive fishing practices or reducing marine protection budgets by more than 20%.

briefing: Dynamite fishing traces found in three restricted reef zones last week.
Ranger patrols need fuel and new interceptors. The old boats are sinking.
Park boundaries are being ignored by liveaboards. Compliance is at an all-time low.
agreement: The reef will thank us. Enforcement without mercy.
I'll mobilize the patrol fleet immediately.
disagreement: You're protecting criminals over coral.
This weakens the marine buffer we've spent decades building.
dialogue.betrayal: You legalized destructive fishing. I've alerted the national marine conservation agency directly.
roast: Your patrols are more decorative than functional.
You couldn't spot a poacher in a tidal pool.
scandal_reaction: I'll secure the perimeter and let the evidence speak for itself.
Paperwork and photos are being sent to Jakarta this morning.

advisors.name: Maria Goretti
advisors.hidden_agenda: Forcing the government to build a massive water desalination plant in her home district.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Awarding the municipal water contract exclusively to the private luxury resorts.

briefing: The resorts have full swimming pools, while our locals are buying water from trucks.
The landfill is overflowing into the forest.
Traffic on the main Marina road is completely paralyzed by tourist buses.
agreement: Finally, development for the people.
A step toward sustainable living.
disagreement: You are building a paradise for tourists and a slum for locals.
This is ecological suicide.
dialogue.betrayal: You gave our water to the hotels. I'm organizing a blockade of the tourist buses tomorrow.
roast: Your vision for this town is just a concrete parking lot with a nice view.
You care more about Instagram aesthetics than basic sanitation.
scandal_reaction: This is the rot at the core of our 'Super Priority' status.
I won't let my name be attached to this mess.

advisors.name: Arum Sinta
advisors.hidden_agenda: Fast-tracking approval for traditional Manggarai-style eco-lodges that use local labor and materials.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Approving concrete high-rise resorts that block traditional sightlines or mandate imported construction materials.

briefing: Traditional stone and bamboo materials are being banned under new fire codes.
The heritage zoning committee needs your signature to block the coastal glass towers.
Eco-lodge permits are stuck in bureaucratic limbo. Local craftsmen are idle.
agreement: Preserves our identity. Sustainable and beautiful.
I'll expedite the heritage compliance waivers.
disagreement: You're erasing Manggarai for generic Jakarta resorts.
This violates our cultural zoning principles.
dialogue.betrayal: You approved concrete monstrosities over our heritage. I'm mobilizing the cultural coalition.
roast: You build skylines but forget the skyline belongs to the mountains.
Concrete doesn't belong in a coral kingdom.
scandal_reaction: This proves we need stricter heritage oversight.
I'll call for an independent architectural review.

advisors.name: Kornelis 'Kony' Jebarus
advisors.hidden_agenda: Monopolizing the lucrative airport-to-harbor shuttle routes for his extended family's cooperative.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Allowing ride-sharing apps like Grab or Gojek to operate freely at the airport and harbor.

briefing: Flights from Bali are delayed again, the airport is chaotic.
The local driver cooperatives are demanding higher fixed rates.
We need to dredge the secondary harbor before the low tide season.
agreement: Mantap, Pak. The drivers are happy.
Smooth sailing from here.
disagreement: You're taking rice from the mouths of our local drivers.
Expect a strike at the airport.
dialogue.betrayal: You brought in the apps. Our drivers are starving. I am leading the strike myself.
roast: This administration is running aground.
You navigate like a blind captain in a storm.
scandal_reaction: I've got a speedboat ready if you need to disappear to the islands.
Things are moving too fast, Pak.

advisors.name: Dimas Prasetyo
advisors.hidden_agenda: Automating harbor ticketing and ferry scheduling to eliminate local operator middlemen.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Banning digital ticketing systems or subsidizing unregulated private boat operators.

briefing: Ferry terminal queues are averaging 4 hours. The manual ticketing system is broken.
Port dredging contracts are delayed again due to cash-flow disputes.
We need a unified digital transit app before the high season peaks.
agreement: Efficiency wins. The system will run smoothly.
I'll push the digital rollout next week.
disagreement: You're disrupting traditional livelihoods without transition plans.
This algorithm ignores local operational realities.
dialogue.betrayal: You banned digital scheduling and returned to cash handoffs. I'm resigning in protest.
roast: You manage traffic like it's 1990.
Your logistics are held together with hope and handshakes.
scandal_reaction: I'll audit the procurement chain immediately.
The data logs will show exactly where the delays started.

advisors.name: Romo Yosef
advisors.hidden_agenda: Reclaiming coastal land inside the National Park for traditional Manggarai indigenous use.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Approving the forced eviction of traditional fishing villages to expand the National Park tourist zones.

briefing: The elders are worried we are losing our cultural identity to mass tourism.
Land disputes between locals and Jakarta developers are turning violent.
We must remember that this land is borrowed from our ancestors.
agreement: Puji Tuhan. A wise and just decision.
The ancestors smile upon this.
disagreement: You are selling our heritage for Rupiah.
This will bring a curse upon the land.
dialogue.betrayal: You evicted our people from their ancestral homes. The Church and the elders stand united against you.
roast: You worship foreign investors as if they were gods.
Your soul is as polluted as the harbor water.
scandal_reaction: We must seek forgiveness for the greed that has infected this town.
The truth cannot be hidden forever.

advisors.name: Kepala Adat Yohanes
advisors.hidden_agenda: Restoring traditional village councils (mufakat) to approve all major land deals before government action.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Bypassing customary village consultations for any major development or land zoning change.

briefing: Three villages refuse to sign new tourism permits without a proper mufakat.
Traditional dispute resolution is being ignored by municipal courts.
The harvest ceremonies need municipal recognition and funding.
agreement: The village speaks as one. Respect for adat.
I'll call the clan heads together tonight.
disagreement: You bypassed the elders. This breaks our ancient agreements.
Paper permits mean nothing without village blessing.
dialogue.betrayal: You trampled on customary law. The mufakat will deny you legitimacy in every district.
roast: You rule by decree while ignoring the roots.
A mayor without the people's trust is just a guest.
scandal_reaction: We must return to the longhouse and speak truth to power.
The elders will convene. Expect a traditional verdict.

decisions.title: The Premium Island Dispute
decisions.body: Jakarta wants to raise the entry fee to Komodo Island to $250 per person to limit mass tourism and protect the dragons. Local tour guides say this will instantly bankrupt their businesses.

options.label: Implement a moderate, staggered price increase and cap daily visitor numbers.

consequences.public_reaction: A compromise. Nobody is happy, but nobody is rioting.

options.label: Fully enforce the $250 premium ticket to attract only high-end tourists.

consequences.public_reaction: You just killed the local backpacker economy overnight!

options.label: Make entry completely free for locals, but charge foreigners $500.

consequences.public_reaction: The foreign embassies are furious, but the locals are cheering.

decisions.title: The Clean Water Divide
decisions.body: A severe dry season has hit. Five-star resorts have their own desalination plants and lush gardens, while local residents are paying extortionate prices for dirty water from roaming tanker trucks.

options.label: Subsidize the local water tanker trucks using municipal funds.

consequences.public_reaction: Thanks for the water, but why is the system still broken?

options.label: Legally mandate the luxury resorts to supply 20% of their desalinated water to the local grid.

consequences.public_reaction: Finally, the rich are sharing the resources they exploit!

options.label: Cut the municipal pipes to the resorts completely until they fund a new public reservoir.

consequences.public_reaction: The tourists are showering with bottled Evian! This is hilarious.

decisions.title: The 'Jurassic Park' Construction
decisions.body: The central government has begun construction on a massive, concrete observation deck on Rinca Island to commercialize dragon viewing. Conservationists are protesting the destruction of the dragons' natural habitat.

options.label: Comply with Jakarta, but deploy local rangers to minimize the environmental impact.

consequences.public_reaction: We are turning a natural wonder into a cheap theme park.

options.label: Defy Jakarta, revoke the building permits, and physically block the construction equipment.

consequences.public_reaction: Save Komodo! The Bupati is actually standing up to the oligarchs!

options.label: Demand a 50% cut of all future ticket sales to fund local infrastructure, or you'll release the dragons.

consequences.public_reaction: Extorting the central government? You have a death wish, Pak.

decisions.title: The Tide of Plastic
decisions.body: Monsoon currents are washing up tons of plastic waste onto the pristine beaches of the National Park. Much of it is dumped illegally by cheap, unregulated liveaboard boats.

options.label: Fund a massive, ongoing cleanup crew using municipal budgets.

consequences.public_reaction: It looks nice today, but it will be back tomorrow.

options.label: Ban single-use plastics and aggressively impound boats caught dumping.

consequences.public_reaction: Harsh, but the only way to save the ocean.

options.label: Collect the trash and dump it outside the Ministry of Tourism's local office in protest.

consequences.public_reaction: Absolute legend! Let Jakarta deal with their own mess!

decisions.title: Coastal Land Disputes
decisions.body: Wealthy investors from Jakarta are buying up premium coastal land from impoverished locals at rock-bottom prices, often using intimidation or exploiting unclear land titles.

options.label: Implement a mandatory 90-day waiting and review period for all coastal land sales.

consequences.public_reaction: A band-aid on a bullet wound, but it's something.

options.label: Nullify recent predatory sales and establish a protected Indigenous Land Trust.

consequences.public_reaction: Protecting our ancestral lands! The developers are furious.

options.label: Approve the sales, but instantly rezone the purchased land as a public waste facility.

consequences.public_reaction: Enjoy building your five-star resort next to the new dump!

crises.name: Harbor Paralyzed by Tour Guide Strike
crises.trigger: Anger over ticket price hikes and monopoly fears has culminated in a massive strike by local guides and boat captains.
crises.description: The harbor is completely blockaded by wooden Phinisi boats. Thousands of tourists are stranded at the airport or in their hotels. International media is running stories of 'Chaos in Paradise.' You have 24 hours to open the port.

options.label: Cave to the demands and permanently roll back the ticket prices.
Deploy the riot police and maritime security to forcibly clear the harbor.
Arrest the strike leaders and bring in non-union operators from Bali to break the strike.

crises.name: Liveaboard Sinks in Current
crises.trigger: An overloaded, unlicensed Phinisi boat has sunk in the treacherous currents near Batu Bolong.
crises.description: Several international tourists are missing. The boat had no proper safety equipment and the captain fled. Foreign embassies are demanding answers, and the safety of the entire tourism industry is being questioned globally.

options.label: Launch a massive, expensive search and rescue op while grounding all boats temporarily.
Use the tragedy to permanently revoke the licenses of all small, locally-owned wooden boats.
Blame the tourists for ignoring safety warnings to protect the local boat industry's reputation.

crises.name: Inferno on Padar Island
crises.trigger: Reckless tourists using flares for a pre-wedding photoshoot have ignited the dry savanna on the iconic Padar Island.
crises.description: The flames are spreading rapidly across the hills, threatening wildlife and destroying the landscape that brings millions of dollars to the region. We have almost no firefighting infrastructure capable of reaching the island quickly.

options.label: Beg the military to deploy water-bombing helicopters from Bali.
Mobilize hundreds of local boatmen and rangers for a dangerous manual firefighting operation.
Let it burn to regenerate the ecosystem naturally, despite the massive PR disaster.

crises.name: Central Government Ultimatum
crises.trigger: Citing chronic mismanagement of the 'Super Priority' destination, Jakarta threatens to revoke local autonomy.
crises.description: The Ministry of Tourism and the central government want to place Labuan Bajo under a special, federally-run authority. This would effectively strip you of all power, turning you into a figurehead while Jakarta controls the budgets and the land.

options.label: Submit to the federal authority to ensure continued infrastructure funding.
Rally the local population in massive protests to defend regional autonomy.
Threaten to close the Komodo National Park completely if Jakarta interferes.

scandals.title: Bupati Uses Patrol Boat for Private Island Party
scandals.description: Photos leaked on Instagram showing you and several developers using a government maritime patrol vessel for a luxury party near Pink Beach.

scandals.title: Bupati's Family Owns Resort Water Supplier
scandals.description: Investigative journalists discovered that the sole company permitted to supply bottled water to the major resorts is secretly owned by your brother-in-law.

scandals.title: Caught on Tape: Selling National Park Borders
scandals.description: A hidden camera recording shows you accepting a massive bribe in a Jakarta hotel to rezone a protected section of the National Park for a mega-resort.

positive: Mantap Pak Bupati! Finally someone looking out for us.
Puji Tuhan, a good decision for the people.
This is why we voted for him.
Semoga Lancar! Let's hope this fixes the traffic.
Good to see the environment being protected for once.
Respect. We need to save Komodo before it's too late.
neutral: Kita lihat saja nanti. Promises are easy.
As long as the tourists keep coming, I guess.
Air mati terus, tapi hotel mewah lancar.
Biasa, orang atas yang untung, kita yang gigit jari.
I'll believe it when the water actually comes out of my tap.
negative: Aduh, Bapak ini bikin pusing kepala saja.
Only caring about the investors from Jakarta!
They are destroying our home for money.
This is a disaster for Labuan Bajo.
We are becoming strangers in our own land.
media: Pos Kupang: Controversial new policy shakes local tourism sector.
Flores Pos: Protests erupt near the harbor over latest regulations.
Kompas: Jakarta closely monitoring Labuan Bajo's management.
The Jakarta Post: Is the 'Super Priority' destination failing its locals?
Local Radio: Callers express frustration over persistent water shortages.
Breaking: Viral video exposes infrastructure failures in paradise.
politician: We must balance economic growth with environmental sustainability.
The central government will not tolerate incompetence in this region.
This is an attack on the livelihood of local workers.
We support the Bupati in these difficult modernizing steps.
The investors need certainty, not chaotic policies.
We demand an investigation into this decision.
activist: Save Komodo! Stop building Jurassic Park!
Water is a human right, not just for five-star hotels!
Protect our indigenous lands from Jakarta oligarchs!
Clean up the ocean! The plastic is killing the reefs!
street: Bro, the traffic to the airport is macet total again.
Air PDAM mati lagi hari ini. Beli tangki lagi kita.
The tourists are complaining about the trash on the beach.
Harga tiket naik, tamu pada batal datang. Mati kita.
Just trying to get enough guests for my boat tomorrow.
Panas sekali hari ini, bikin emosi.
crisis: Tuhan kasihanilah kami! This is terrible!
The harbor is completely blocked! No one can leave!
Jakarta is going to take over, we are finished.
scandal: Tangkap dan penjarakan! Corrupt to the bone.
They steal our land and party on our boats.
We are a joke to the rest of Indonesia.
KPK (Anti-Corruption Agency) needs to arrest him immediately.

scandal_reactions.moderate: They are drinking our water while we pay for it. Memalukan!
scandal_reactions.major: Bring the KPK down here right now! Arrest him!
scandal_reactions.career_ending: Selesai sudah. You are going to wearing the orange vest in Jakarta.

romance_exposure.flavour: In a strongly Catholic and traditional society like Manggarai, the scandal causes massive outrage among church leaders and traditional elders, severely damaging your moral standing.
